items.title: Asia's great disconnect: Markets boom as Iran war fuels cost of living crisis
Middle East crisis live: Donald Trump cancels US envoy trip to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran
items.description: US president says ‘too much time’ has been ‘wasted on traveling’ as Israel strikes Lebanon, claiming Hezbollah also launched rocketsWe have some images coming through the newswires of Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, speaking with Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, and other officials in Islamabad this morning.…

items.title: Netanyahu, 'ho ordinato all'Idf di attaccare con vigore Hezbollah in Libano'
items.description: L''ufficio del premier israeliano: 'A seguito delle varie violazioni del cessate il fuoco'

items.title: Syria to begin trying Assad-era figures Sunday, justice ministry official says
items.description: Cases against security officials and militia leaders accused of atrocities signal first test for Syria’s new justice system The post Syria to begin trying Assad-era figures Sunday, justice ministry official says appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Joy and desire for change as Gaza’s Deir el-Balah holds elections
items.description: Deir el-Balah residents vote for first time since 2006 despite destruction, displacement and genocide.

items.title: NGOs call for a global response to US sanctions against ICC
items.description: A coalition of 2,000 NGOs published a report mapping the US sanctions system targeting the International Criminal Court and urging member states, the European Union and the United Nations to respond firmly to measures that also penalize those working to protect human rights.

items.title: Mali hit by wave of coordinated attacks from armed groups
items.description: Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

items.title: Russian Strikes Kill 6 Across Ukraine
items.description: Four people were killed and 27 wounded in a single strike on an apartment block in Dnipro. Another strike nearly killed a local official.

items.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
items.description: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

items.title: Mali live : Army clashes with ‘terrorist’ groups, Kidal captured, Tuareg rebels say
items.description: Gunmen attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities early Saturday. Mali’s army said its soldiers were “engaged in eliminating the attackers”. Meanwhile, Tuareg rebels of the Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) coalition said they had captured the northern city of Kidal. Follow our liveblog for the latest up…

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah Exchange Strikes Despite Three-Week Truce Extension
items.description: At least six people were killed in southern Lebanon as both sides traded fire and accused each other of violating the ceasefire announced by US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Amid extended ceasefire, Lebanon assesses high cost of Israel-Hezbollah war
items.description: In this latest round of Israel-Hezbollah fighting, Lebanese civilians have once again paid a heavy price. Even as the ceasefire in Lebanon is extended, neither combatant is showing signs of backing down.

items.title: Ukraine: Zelenskyy visits Saudi Arabia, Putin weighs G20 attendance
items.description: Russian President Putin "may or not may not" attend the G20 in Miami in December, says the Kremlin. Meanwhile, after securing an EU loan, Ukrainian President Zelenskyy continued his diplomatic offensive in Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Zelenskyy In Saudi Arabia For Defense Talks As Russia Continues To Pound Ukraine
items.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Saudi Arabia for security talks, marking his second visit to the region since the start of the US-Israeli war with Iran, now under cease-fire, as Russia continued to pound Ukraine with deadly strikes.

items.title: World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update
items.description: Continued strikes, shelling and gunfire in residential areas of Gaza are raising alarm over civilian safety, the UN relief coordination office OCHA has warned.
